review_text:
Most dysfunctional MRT station.

The manager in charge of the station is extremely rude and enjoys locking up commuters’ bicycles parked for 48 hours with his personal bike lock.

The train arrival ETA screen doesn’t work half the time.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Nearby HK or is Zhi chat restaurant (Wang Jiao) serve really nice wohei fried rice. Is super near to mrt. Served for private residents. Saw a famous prata shop too n recommended by frd but didn't get to try

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Spring Leaf MRT station is clean, modern, and well-maintained, providing a convenient connection for residents in the northern part of Singapore. The station is spacious with clear signage, making it easy to navigate even during peak hours. Train services are generally smooth and efficient. The surrounding area is still developing, so amenities are somewhat limited compared to more established stations, but accessibility and connectivity are excellent. Overall, a welcome addition that makes commuting much more convenient for nearby residents.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Opposite the Springleaf Prata place. Very near woodlands. And walkable to Khatib through the nearby park connector.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Nice clean station with wide accessible walkways much like all the other stations across Singapore. Loved the vending machine with the souvenir trains and buses available to purchase on the way to the exit for the zoo's.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Adventure HQ is the biggest indoor obstacle course in Singapore and a very fun, engaging experience for both adults and kids. The staff are friendly, professional, and take safety seriously, making the whole activity feel well-managed and reassuring. The obstacles provide a good level of challenge without being overwhelming, so it’s enjoyable for different fitness levels. It takes about 4 hours to complete all the activities, making it a great half-day adventure. Highly recommended to book in advance.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Had a fun time at Adventure HQ. Previously did something similar at Action Motion but i’d say that this has much more elements compared to Action Motion.

Staffs were friendly and kind to assist my partner and I, as well as, the really largeee group. Hands down to all those that worked on 14/6/26 slot 10-2.30pm (specifically Athena, Khilfi, Auni, Syafiq). Also, thankful that we were able to proceed despite coming late.

Overall, enjoyed my time here. Personally for me, i’d rate the difficulty of these elements/course a 2/10. Experience definitely 10/10.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs

review_text: The park itself is fine if you don't intend to go onto the other side of the waterway because the number of bridges linking the two sides are few and far apart. I am not sure why it is designed like that. We happened to be on the opposite side of the "main attractions" like Waterway Point, SAFRA, etc and we just could not get over easily. I guess if you are aiming to have lots of steps, this is the place to be.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Yishun library is located at the 4th level of Yishun Northpoint City (North wing). A modern designed and cosy place, but is smaller size as compared to the other libraries being single level. Few sitting areas and you may have difficulty booking library seats during the peak seasons (school holidays & public holidays). Can be noisy at times. I like the quiet reading lounge area but it is often fully occupied. The library carries diverse collections of themed books, such as comics,  animation and illustration, which are great for the children. Several multimedia and digital stations make access to digital content and e-services conveniently. One feedback: you need to scan to enter the library and the queue is often jammed at the entrance - an area to consider for improvement.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Been coming here a lot. Love the place and the small reading nooks. It can get crowded with a lot of students but plenty of spaces you can find. I live that their collection of fiction books is always updated and the shelves not overfilled.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Yishun Library moved over to Northpoint lv 4 since ages ago from the Blk 290 near where POSB Yishun North branch is located. Been here multiple times, it’s clean and very cosy to be. As a non-reader I still find it fascinating and fun to visit a library to browse through interesting books and comics.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:

review_text:
Enrolled my 2 boys, 2yo & 4yo early this year and they have adapted very well. Special thanks to Ms Jane whom is very experienced and passionate when it comes to teaching the little children. Along with Yuan laoshi & Sun laoshi. Li laoshi & Ms Nena are also my children’s class teacher. The school has a holistic curriculum planning and provides weekly update on their classroom learning. The principal, Ms Anne & admin, Charis are also very friendly and approachable.
Thumbs up to all the aunties & staff at Little Seeds(Praiseland).

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
🌿Yishun Neighbourhood Park Is located up the hillock at the junction of Yishun Ave 2 and Yishun Ave 7.
🌳The Park is a former rubber plantation.
🌱Up at the quiet park summit is an Open Lawn suitable for outdoor activities.
🛝The Park features a Children Playground, a multi-generation Fitness Corner, and an Allotment Garden which were all located at the hilltop.
👣A slightly sloped Foot Reflexology Path lies on the slope of the hillock near the summit.
🏋️There is also a Gym Pod just across the Fitness Corner.
🌹The entire Park is clean and peaceful with plenty of greenery and beautiful flowering plants.
🧘Residents living nearby visit the park in the morning for their morning exercises.
🍀Yishun Neighbourhood Park is among the several town parks around Yishun area.
♦️Fun note:
Yishun is the chinese hanyu pinyin for Née Soon, derived from Lim Née Soon, a prominent businessman and philanthropist, who had large area of his rubber and pineapple plantations located here in Yishun area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Neighborhood park, crowded in the morning & evening as many peoples exercise here.

Small park but well developed for walk/jog, exercise; it’s also small playgroup suitable for kids. In the middle of the park have place for planting too.

Personally I think not really suitable for relax bike ride as is up slopes all the way 😂

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
On a green space Singapore arc

This place is tranquail. Probably the highest natural space in Yishun, definitely most enlightened being there.

The residents nearby love exercising her,e, and I love how this park is littered with pathways spiralling both upward and downwards. The circular tracks makes the layout of the park quite intriguing for a first time. People I bump into, I bump into them again after a while because of the circular effect of the spiralling pathways.

A great green space.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A good bowl of Lormee should have that thick, gooey yet smooth gravy and packed with flavour. Unfortunately, this Lormee at Yishun Park (早碗见面 Lor mee & Satay Beehoon) , gravy was diluted and has no umami taste.

Even after adding vinegar, garlic and chilli, it’s still pretty one dimensional. The only saving grace was their fried fish, nicely fried, soft and still has that slight crunchy exterior.

Damage: $4.80 (small bowl)

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Family went there for lunch on a Saturday at around 1130H. It was not too crowded, and we managed to find a table without needing to wait. Parking was okay too.

The hawker centre was big and airy, we didn't feel hot. The tables were not so clean, and no one seemed to be going around to wipe the tables too.

We ordered (1) fish sliced soup, (2) Ming jian Kueh, (3) kids nasi lemak meal, (4) ice kachang.

Food is pretty much the standard stuff... just two things to say - (1) Shrinkflation is real - could have more fish slices in the soup, and (2) nasi lemak rice was fragrant but the chicken fillet was dry and tasteless.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Serene park with a PCN connecting to Lower Seletar Reservior Park. Good place for bird watching as I could hear them singing. Spotted a monitor lizard swimming leisurely in the canal, and a monkey minding its own business on the railings. A few youngsters fishing, some folks cycling, most people walking and jogging, and some photographers with their telephoto cameras.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Beautiful park, greenery and animals. Nature at its finest. Also nearby the restaurant 'Three'. Home to various pond ecosystem creatures such as Monkeys, otters, dragonflies, terrapin and more!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Good trail along the river but do note there is not much shade. Be prepared to be exposed to the sun.

Also be on the look out for the monitor lizards taking a lazy swim in the middle of the river. Quite a sight!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Accessible from Springleaf MRT after a short walk. Beautiful in the morning.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Serene and peaceful park with lots of beautiful mature trees and plants. Situated next to a canal that is connected to the Lower Seletar Reservoir, there are plenty of wildlife to look out for, including squirrel,  monitor lizard, and even an occasional green snake. A good place to immerse in nature for a walk and run.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

review_text:
cannot say enough good things about Small Wonder Yishun Adora Green]! From the moment we first walked through the doors, we felt welcomed, supported, and confident that our child was in the best hands possible. The teachers are incredibly caring, patient, and genuinely invested in every child’s growth — not just academically, but emotionally and socially too.
The learning environment is bright, clean, and filled with creativity. Our child comes home excited to share something new — a song they learned, an art project they made, or a fun story from circle time. The balance between play-based learning and structured activities is perfect; it fosters curiosity while building important early skills.
Communication with parents is excellent as well. We receive regular updates, photos, and progress reports, so we always know how our child is doing and what they’re learning. It’s clear that the entire staff truly loves what they do, and that makes all the difference.
We’ve seen such wonderful growth in our child’s confidence, independence, and love of learning since joining Small Wonder Yishun Adora Green. We are so grateful to have found this amazing place and would recommend it wholeheartedly to any family looking for a nurturing, joyful, and high-quality early education experience.

review_text:
Came here just because TEL thomson MRT will start operation at 8.30am on every Saturdays from 22 june to 4 july.

My job starts at 7am , so i had to take bus 855 from opp khatib bus stop. Luvkily there is an over bridge just nearby Giant hypermarket. There is a HUGE signboard what buses are available, so it is easy to find my way there.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One of our earlier MRTs with a simple,  grassroots vibe. And with a Giant of its own,  which is a different vibe again from the ubiquitous NTUCs which seem to be taking over ever other time.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Khatib MRT is one of the busiest MRT stations in Singapore, offering excellent connectivity and accessibility. Located near Yishun MRT and Yio Chu Kang MRT, it serves as a key transit point for both daily commuters and visitors. Compared to other MRT stations, Khatib is one of the older yet well-developed stations, making it a significant landmark in the area.

One of the unique aspects of Khatib MRT is its connection to the HDB residential area. To reach the HDB section, commuters need to cross a passenger flyover, which provides a clear view of the surrounding area, including a frequently used lift.

Additionally, Khatib MRT is a major access point to Singapore Zoo, with a dedicated shuttle service running between the station and the zoo. This makes the station particularly crowded, especially on weekends and holidays, as families and tourists use it to reach the zoo conveniently.

Within the same building as Khatib MRT, there is a Giant supermarket, offering easy access to groceries and daily essentials—an added convenience for residents and travelers. The area also features several food shops, serving delicious local cuisine, making it a great spot to grab a meal.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: AMK Hub is a really convenient neighbourhood mall, especially since it’s directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT and the bus interchange. It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Lots of shops and less shopping shops.only essential shops are available like daily use stuffs. More favorite thing attracted me it the play area next to jollie bee food court

It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Lots of shops and less shopping shops.only essential shops are available like daily use stuffs. More favorite thing attracted me it the play area next to jollie bee food court

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Did not personally visit the gym but wanted to give my sincerest thanks to their wonderful staff Adelin and Fariz (apologies if I misspelled your names), who helped my father during a bad fall outside the gym premise. Both staff members took prompt action upon seeing his fall and immediately brought a first aid kit and oxygen mask, reassuring my father while we were waiting for the ambulance. Thank you again 🙏

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Big field with alot of activities that I can do there, the gym is usually packed and it is right beside a swimming pool so I can cool off after a gym session. However, the distance taken to walk there is very long as it is very far away from woodlands mrt so that's why it's not a 5 star.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Nov 2021 update: just renovated. Feel much larger than before. Only 1 toilet inside the gym, and 2 changing room though. Looks more modern.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Because of the rude and uneducated staff (old man),he made the Active SG image very bad and ugly

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Excellent place to do your laps. They have a nice locker room, lockers for hire at 40 cents a go, benches to lay down on or place your stuff. The life guards are always watching out for you and I felt safe doing my weekly swim there. Do follow the ground rules though to go with the flow.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Where I used to hang out a lot when I was younger. Besides their water cooler much further away from the basketball court now, the basketball court was less accessible due to the more frequent activities occupying them.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Standard cc, nowadays converted into vaccination centre. The design is very nice. Indoor area but the walls are actually just a lot of small pillars so does not feel v stuffy. There is a fast food place inside, which is probably more useful when ppl actually use the cc for sports and other physical activities. They do provide the usual admin support for elderly, which is very helpful.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The building interior appears to be quite old, and the space does not seem to be effectively utilised. It feels like it could be better planned, but the SAFRA membership is very affordable.

One good thing is that there is a McDonald’s here. It is convenient for drive-through orders, and the interior of the McDonald’s outlet is quite nice and up to date too.

There is also an EnergyOne gym (need to sign up for membership / per entry) on Level 2, above the swimming pool area, which is convenient for people living nearby. The gym is decent and well-equipped, and it comes with shower facilities, a sauna, and a jacuzzi (located at the Level 1 swimming pool, but accessible only through the gym).

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Many activities at Safra Yishun for family and a swimming facility with 5 pools including kids and jacuzzi 😘🤩

Female toilet at swimming pool needs to upkeep as flushing is always difficult and the tap is spoilt and has a musty smell.

review_text:

Recently they upgraded their bbq pit and it has new chairs and tables!!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Lower Seletar Reservoir Park is such a peaceful and scenic spot in Singapore! 🌅 The waterfront views are beautiful, especially during sunrise and sunset, and the park is very well maintained. It’s perfect for evening strolls, jogging, cycling, or just relaxing by the water. The jetty and heritage boardwalk add a special charm, and the cool breeze from the reservoir makes it even more refreshing. A great place to unwind and enjoy nature — highly recommended! 🌿✨

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
10/10, would absolutely paddle back for this again. 🌅🚣‍♂️

The sunrise kayak + breakfast tour was such a peaceful, feel-good start to the day. Ali and Shiva were fantastic, friendly, funny, and genuinely informative. They made everything feel easy and welcoming, and were always ready to help while keeping the whole experience light and enjoyable.

It’s one of those rare activities that’s genuinely for everyone. Easy to pick up, surprisingly calming, and somehow makes you feel like you’ve got your life together before 9am. Paddle a little, enjoyed a lot, then reward yourself with breakfast by the water… what’s not to love?

Seas the day, eat your way through it, repeat. Try it!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
The park is beautifully maintained, with a delightful variety of flowers that add color and charm to every corner. Each pathway feels inviting, filled with vibrant blooms and natural scents that make the walk feel almost therapeutic.

The gardens are especially peaceful—perfect for an evening stroll after a long day. Whether you want to unwind, enjoy nature, or simply spend some quiet time alone, this park offers the ideal atmosphere. It’s a serene escape from the noise of the city, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone looking for a calm and scenic place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
A Serene Escape, Even in a Light Drizzle

The Equatorial showers in this heartland park is a gem, especially in light rain. I recently went for a walk as a gentle shower began, and it was a surprisingly perfect experience. The rain kept the crowds away, and the sound of drops on the canopy and the reservoir's surface was incredibly peaceful.

The best part was watching the water itself. Each raindrop created a cascade of tiny, expanding ripples across the entire reservoir. It was a mesmerizing and deeply calming effect, turning the whole scene into a living, moving work of art.

It’s a wonderful reminder that you don't need to travel far for a moment of tranquility. A short, refreshing walk here, even under a light rain, is a perfect way to reset.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Yishun MRT Station, located on Singapore’s North-South Line (Red Line), is one of the most convenient and well-connected MRT stations in the northern region. Whether you’re commuting daily or just visiting the area, Yishun MRT offers excellent accessibility and a smooth travel experience.

One of the standout features of Yishun MRT is its strategic location. Just next to the station is the large and popular Northpoint City Mall, which makes this area a vibrant hub of shopping, dining, and services. The mall has a wide variety of retail outlets, supermarkets, food courts, restaurants, electronics shops, fashion stores, and essential services like banks, clinics, and salons—all within walking distance from the station.

Right outside the MRT station is the Yishun Bus Interchange, which connects to many bus routes across Singapore. This makes it extremely convenient for commuters to transfer between MRT and bus services with ease. Whether you’re heading to other parts of the North, or travelling toward the city, Yishun serves as a great starting point or transfer hub.

The station itself is clean, well-maintained, and safe, with helpful signage and barrier-free access for elderly and wheelchair users. During peak hours, it gets quite busy, but the crowd is managed well.

In terms of convenience, everything you need is nearby—groceries, food, retail shopping, public transport, and even community facilities. It’s an ideal location for residents and visitors alike.

Overall, Yishun MRT Station is not just a transport node—it’s a central hub for lifestyle and daily needs in the northern part of Singapore. If you’re looking for easy commuting with all amenities nearby, this is one of the best-connected and most practical MRT stations in the city.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Yishun MRT Station in Singapore is a busy yet well-connected transport hub on the North-South Line that links seamlessly with the Yishun Bus Interchange and Northpoint City mall, offering commuters easy access to shopping, residential, and community areas, but despite its convenience, accessibility, and integration, it faces recurring challenges such as heavy crowding during peak hours, occasional escalator breakdowns, cleanliness issues around bicycle parking zones, and periodic service disruptions due to ageing infrastructure—making it both a vital and sometimes frustrating centerpiece of daily life in northern Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I had a truly wonderful time at Springleaf Park! 🏞️ The atmosphere was so refreshing, and it was the perfect spot for a delightful outing. The serene surroundings and beautiful greenery made for such a pleasant experience. I'm definitely looking forward to my next visit! 😊🌳✨

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Lovely park connector — peaceful, clean and great for a relaxing walk or cycling. Lots of greenery and well-maintained paths. A nice escape from the busy city!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
🍀Springleaf Park Connector connect Upper Thomson Road to Lentor Avenue.
🍀The entire Park Connector runs side by side to Sungei Seletar.
🚦Née Soon Road cuts through the Springleaf Park Connector, so you need to cross the road to continue on.
🍀As you stroll through this lush Park Connector, you can hear the constant bird calls, testimony to its rich biodiversity.
🐾It's a jogging track for the nearby residents.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Nice short PCN connecting Lentor Avenue to Upper Thomson Road. Good for a short morning walk before breakfast. Within 5 minutes walk from Springleaf MRT Station.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Since the opening of the Springleaf PCN between 2014 to 2015 , it has been one of my cycling and walking routes to cross over Lentor Avenue to Upper Thomson Road & vice versa. For bird watchers, it's one of the spots in SG. One thing for sure, I will never failed to see the water lilies in the Seletar River.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Runningkids Yishun Student Care & Learning Hub
review_text: Very good student care. Especially they promote Chinese learning and reading, my girl really enjoys RK programs. Now she takes higher Chinese in P3 and she can read Chinese books independently. RK also conduct English lessons by MoE teachers. I never need to worry about her academic performance, sometimes she brings surprise tome. RK teachers are very supportive and give me peace of mind. Highly recommend!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Runningkids Yishun Student Care & Learning Hub
review_text: Ms. Zhou and Mr. Ho are truly professional and passionate about teaching. They put in great effort to highlight the most important topics, concepts and applications, ensuring students build a strong foundation. The classes are interactive and engaging, which makes learning enjoyable and effective. My child is always motivated to improve and has shown steady progress under the teachers’ guidance. Highly recommended for both Chinese and Science tuition!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Runningkids Yishun Student Care & Learning Hub
review_text:
From Primary 1 through now his PSLE year, Running Kids has been more than just student care - it's been a second family for our son. The warm, nurturing environment and dedicated teachers created the perfect place for him to grow both academically and personally.

As an English-speaking household, we were especially amazed by Zhou Lao Shi's remarkable impact. Through her patient and engaging Chinese lessons (P5-P6 currently), she transformed what was once a challenging subject into his current passion! Her teaching brought incredible improvement, from initial struggles to now confidently conversing in Mandarin at home and genuinely enjoying the language.

A special 谢谢 to Zhou Lao Shi for making learning so joyful and effective.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Runningkids Yishun Student Care & Learning Hub
review_text: Runningkids Student Care has taken great care of my daughter for the past 2 years. She enjoys the environment, and has made good friends. Her English has improved tremendously in her time there as a result of the in-house tuition provided. They have also been extremely accommodating and responsive with ad-hoc requests across the years and communication with the staff has always been positive. 10/10 would recommend.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Runningkids Yishun Student Care & Learning Hub
review_text: My son joined P6 Chinese and Science tuition lessons. My son enjoyed the lessons very much. Teachers are very caring and meticulous in teaching. Materials are great. Am happy with my son’s significant improvement in Chinese and Science grades in prelim PSLE. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Visited Chong Pang Hawker Centre recently and tried a few stalls… overall a really satisfying meal.

Started with Chuan Kee Boneless Braised Duck Rice. The duck was very tender and flavourful, with a nice herbal braised sauce that wasn’t too overpowering. I also liked that it was boneless, which made it easy to eat.

Then I had the char kway teow from Pin Xiang. Good wok hei, noodles were well-cooked and not overly greasy. Portion was decent and the flavours were balanced.

To finish, I got an ice blended watermelon juice from Fruit Palace. Very refreshing, especially in the heat… not too sweet and quite natural tasting.

Overall, a solid hawker experience. Would come back to try other stalls as well 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A wet market and hawker centre located in a local area under an HDB. People are very nice and welcoming. My go to every week for fresh fruit and vegetables. Hawker foods are amazing, from Chinese to Indian and every in between. Michelin Cendol here too and it is a must try!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Good old hawker with a mox of variety of food choices. The famous Chew Kuah, Duck Rice, Fish soup and Porridge, delicious Nyonya Chendol are just a few to mention. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Food lover can consider this places. But prepared to sweat if the weather turn hot! Today had tried this prawns noodle stall. First taste of the soup bring me back to my childhood life. Soup very authentic,  ingredients very fresh. And for the pork slice,  cooked to perfection tender.. I will come back for more . Alot of nice food too. Wish my stomach can eat from every stall. Hehehe. Only ＄4 for small bowl. But is more than enough for me.

Feb 25, this market is huge and plenty of food . Try the fish soup 01-147-‘s the Nonya Chendol.
Have the fish soup again, very good plus the chendol.
Try the Hainanese Chicken rice set at $5.80 plus a chicken wings - $8.10. Very good. Recommended to try.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Visited Chong Pang Hawker Centre recently and tried a few stalls… overall a really satisfying meal.

Started with Chuan Kee Boneless Braised Duck Rice. The duck was very tender and flavourful, with a nice herbal braised sauce that wasn’t too overpowering. I also liked that it was boneless, which made it easy to eat.

Then I had the char kway teow from Pin Xiang. Good wok hei, noodles were well-cooked and not overly greasy. Portion was decent and the flavours were balanced.

To finish, I got an ice blended watermelon juice from Fruit Palace. Very refreshing, especially in the heat… not too sweet and quite natural tasting.

Overall, a solid hawker experience. Would come back to try other stalls as well 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A wet market and hawker centre located in a local area under an HDB. People are very nice and welcoming. My go to every week for fresh fruit and vegetables. Hawker foods are amazing, from Chinese to Indian and every in between. Michelin Cendol here too and it is a must try!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Good old hawker with a mox of variety of food choices. The famous Chew Kuah, Duck Rice, Fish soup and Porridge, delicious Nyonya Chendol are just a few to mention. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Food lover can consider this places. But prepared to sweat if the weather turn hot! Today had tried this prawns noodle stall. First taste of the soup bring me back to my childhood life. Soup very authentic,  ingredients very fresh. And for the pork slice,  cooked to perfection tender.. I will come back for more . Alot of nice food too. Wish my stomach can eat from every stall. Hehehe. Only ＄4 for small bowl. But is more than enough for me.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mustafa Centre is an incredible shopping destination! I always find exactly what I need there, and the variety of items available is truly amazing. 😊 The atmosphere is lively, and it's a great place to explore. So many unique finds! 🛍️ Love it! ✨

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I have been to Singapore several times, but it was my first time going there.
I really regret that I didn't go there before.
There are many snacks, accessories, cosmetics and so on.
As a tourist, I could have reasonable souvenirs.
I will go there next time when I visit Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mammoth! You name it and you'll have it. It spans across the street and variety. Reasonable prices and lot of variety. They have every retail brand under the sky here. Electronics, apparels, grocery, fresh produce, jewellery what not. Hats off to the management. If you are looking for best prices in Singapore this is the place to go.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Yishun | Student Care Yishun
review_text:
My kid joined PlayFACTO Yishun earlier this year and we’re really happy we did. There were a few small hiccups at the start (normal with any new place), but the team sorted them out fast and actually listened to our feedback — not just lip service.

Teachers are warm but firm when they need to be, which is honestly what kids this age need. My child looks forward to going, homework gets done properly, and we always know what’s going on. Big weight off our shoulders as working parents.

Highly recommend 👍

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Yishun | Student Care Yishun
review_text: Came for a centre tour today, Playfacto A posh hub is spacious, clean environment. The admin team and teachers are friendly and answered my queries patiently.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Yishun | Student Care Yishun
review_text: Very comfortable environment and friendly teachers. I have my 3rd child with them. All been well taken care off. Now they are shifting to a bigger place at APosh Bizhub, more spacious and nearer to school.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Yishun | Student Care Yishun
review_text:
I had a very unpleasant experience with PlayFacto at Yishun (A'Posh Bizhub) today.

The students there seemed to have very little awareness of their surroundings and were extremely noisy in the enclosed lobby area of A'Posh Bizhub. While I understand that this is a student care centre and not a formal school, basic discipline and awareness should still be taught. The teachers should be guiding the students on how to behave appropriately in shared spaces.

One incident in particular was quite concerning. I entered the lift and waited about 3–5 seconds before closing the door. During that time, nobody entered, nor did the teachers said anything. But, the moment the door's about to close, a few kids suddenly dashed towards the lift. If I had not quickly held the door open, one of them could easily have been hurt.

After I held the door, a teacher wearing a dark blue dress guided a few children into the lift. One of the kids even joked, “OMG you almost died,” which clearly shows how undisciplined these childrens are and how little the teachers here cares. Afterwards, the teacher did not address the behaviour at all. Instead, she simply walked in, gave me a hostile stare, and said “shhh” to the children. There was no apology, no thank you for holding the door, and above all else, no attempt to correct the child’s behaviour despite the obvious safety risk.

This kind of response felt extremely irresponsible and unprofessional. If this is the standard of supervision and behaviour being tolerated, I would not feel confident recommending this centre to anyone.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: PlayFACTO School @ Yishun | Student Care Yishun
review_text: Visited the open house at Playfacto@APosh Bizhub. The centre was spacious, and the staff were friendly and welcoming. Was impressed by the programme offered by the centre and also the facilities available.

review_text: Boarded bus 811 at blk 309 and had to alight for a short while outside of northpoint to give way to alighting passengers and was met with a rude condescending tone from the bus driver as if i didnt tap my card earlier when i boarded the bus , honestly i would have let this go but the way he told me off is as if i did not pay up for the ride please check your cctv if needed. Not the first time this has happen probably won’t be the last.
